prompt: Which proposed agent action has the highest operational and security risk?

options.text: Format a Markdown file on a feature branch
Summarize a public issue
Rotate production signing keys across services
Add unit-test comments

explanation: Production signing-key rotation can disrupt services and affects highly sensitive credentials. It demands tightly scoped authorization, controlled execution, and human oversight.

prompt: Why should a security-critical HTTP preToolUse hook not be the only control blocking direct pushes?

options.text: HTTP hook network errors and timeouts fall through to the normal permission flow, so repository rules should enforce the final policy
HTTP hooks can only observe completed tool calls
Rulesets disable all hooks
Hooks cannot inspect tool arguments

explanation: GitHub documents HTTP preToolUse hooks as fail-open on network errors, timeouts, and non-2xx responses. A ruleset or branch protection rule provides destination-level enforcement even when that advisory layer is unavailable.

prompt: A deployment agent only needs to update one service in staging. Which two permissions best apply least privilege? Select two.

options.text: A short-lived credential scoped to that service and staging environment
Access only to the deployment operation it must invoke
An organization-owner token
Write access to every production repository

explanation: Least privilege limits both identity scope and available operations. Broad organization or production access creates unnecessary blast radius.

prompt: A production environment requires review, but the person who starts a deployment must not approve it. Which setting enforces that separation?

options.text: Prevent self-review on the protected environment
Allow all MCP servers
Enable force pushes
Increase artifact retention

explanation: Protected environments can require reviewers and prevent the deployment initiator from approving their own job, preserving separation of duties.

prompt: Which two decisions should remain human-owned even when an agent supplies analysis? Select two.

options.text: Approve an exception to a regulatory retention requirement
Resolve an ambiguous, irreversible production-data deletion request
Rename a local test variable
Summarize a completed test run

explanation: Regulatory exceptions and ambiguous irreversible changes require accountable human judgment. Low-risk, reversible development tasks can usually be automated under normal review controls.

prompt: Which approval model best preserves delivery speed without weakening meaningful safeguards?

options.text: Require manual approval for every read operation
Auto-allow low-risk reversible actions that pass policy checks, and require approval only when defined risk thresholds are crossed
Allow every action and review incidents later
Disable automation for all production-adjacent repositories

explanation: Risk-tiered autonomy avoids low-value approval queues while retaining explicit review for actions with material operational, security, compliance, or irreversibility risk.
